query: Virgin Galactic (SPCE) financing, capital raise, cash position and going concern status in 2026. Has the company raised significant capital or secured funding in Q2 2026 (April-June 2026)? What is their current cash runway?
answer:
I've completed the research on Virgin Galactic's (SPCE) financing, cash position, and going concern status. Here's the summary:

## Key Findings

**Going Concern: CONFIRMED.** Virgin Galactic's Q1 2026 10-Q (filed May 14, 2026) contains an explicit ASC 205-40 going concern warning. Management concluded there is "substantial doubt about the Company's ability to continue as a going concern" and — critically — that its mitigation plans do NOT alleviate that doubt (since commercial-service launch and capital-market access are outside its control).

**Cash Position (March 31, 2026):**
- Cash & equivalents: $124.8M + marketable securities: $95.1M + restricted cash: $30.6M
- Headline "cash + marketable securities" ~$251M (~$220M excluding restricted cash), down from ~$338M at year-end 2025.

**Cash Runway: ~2–3 quarters.** Free cash flow burn is ~$87–93M/quarter (Q1 2026 FCF was $(93.3)M; Q2 guided to $(87)M–$(92)M). Against ~$220M liquid assets, that's roughly two to three quarters — the direct basis for the going concern flag.

**Did they raise significant capital in Q2 2026 (April–June)? Not in the traditional cash sense.** The company avoided a large cash equity raise and instead used:
- **ATM equity:** ~$11M in Q1 + ~$52M in April 2026 (modest fresh cash)
- **June 10:** Debt-for-equity — $30.5M First Lien Notes redeemed by issuing 6.73M shares (no cash out)
- **June 22–29:** Exchanged ~$52.5M of 2027 Convertible Notes for stock/warrants, cutting those notes ~75% (from $70.4M to $17.9M)
- New S-3 shelf (~$40M) registered for future ATM sales

These moves **strengthen the balance sheet and cut debt/interest but bring in little fresh operating cash and are heavily dilutive** (shares rose from ~73M to ~81M+ and continued climbing). Survival hinges on launching commercial spaceflight in Q4 2026. The stock traded ~$2.57 on July 10, down ~71% from its 52-week high.

Full structured findings with all data points and sources have been submitted.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic 10-Q Liquidity and Financial Condition (Going Concern) - Q1 2026
sources.snippet: Management concluded the Company may not have sufficient cash and marketable securities to maintain planned operations for the next twelve months; substantial doubt about ability to continue as a going concern; mitigation plans do not alleviate doubt.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic 10-Q Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets - March 31, 2026
sources.snippet: Cash and cash equivalents $124,837K; Restricted cash $30,634K; Marketable securities $95,054K; Total stockholders' equity $223,729K; net cash used in operating activities $(53,501)K.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic Q1 2026 results cut loss, cash burn | SPCE 8-K Filing
sources.snippet: Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities totaled $251M as of March 31, 2026. Raised $11M via 4.0M ATM shares in Q1 and about $52M more in April; Q2 2026 FCF guided $(87)M to $(92)M.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic 8-K - First Lien Notes Partial Redemption (June 10, 2026)
sources.snippet: On June 10, 2026 redeemed $30,524,000 principal of 9.80% First Lien Notes by issuing 6,734,960 shares; ~$172M First Lien Notes remained, no principal due until March 31, 2028.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic 8-K - 2027 Convertible Note Exchange (June 22, 2026)
sources.snippet: Exchange of ~$52.5M of 2.50% Convertible Senior Notes due 2027 for shares/pre-funded warrants, reducing 2027 Notes ~75% from $70.4M to $17.9M; expected to close ~June 29, 2026.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic (NYSE: SPCE) flags going concern risk despite smaller loss | 10-Q
sources.snippet: Management explicitly concluded there is substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern under accounting guidance.

sources.title: SPCE Stock Jumps As Virgin Galactic Extends Cash Runway
sources.snippet: $30.5M debt-for-equity swap retired part of 9.80% notes and pushed remaining principal to 2028; Jefferies reiterated Buy with $5 target citing near-term funding window.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic: Reddit Hype And An Uncertain Liquidity Situation (NYSE:SPCE)
sources.snippet: SPCE faces substantial going concern risks, with only $219 million in liquid assets and quarterly cash burn near $90 million, implying less than three quarters of runway.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic Secures Shareholder Settlement and Investor Backing
sources.snippet: Rich Huang/RichRich Capital disclosed 5.26% stake (5.58M shares); $52.5M convertible note swap reduces 2027 notes ~75%; shareholder litigation settlement preliminary approval ~$2.75M from insurers.

sources.title: Virgin Galactic Stock 2026: SPCE Doubles, Key Trader Watchpoints
sources.snippet: SPCE closed June 1, 2026 at $7.52, up 125% in five trading sessions, after a Q1 EPS and revenue beat and reports of a new strategic investor.

key_facts: Virgin Galactic's Q1 2026 10-Q (filed May 14, 2026) contains an explicit going concern warning under ASC 205-40; management concluded substantial doubt exists and its mitigation plans do NOT alleviate that doubt.

Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities were ~$251M as of March 31, 2026 (cash & equivalents $124.8M + marketable securities $95.1M + restricted cash $30.6M), down from ~$338M at Dec 31, 2025.
Excluding restricted cash, liquid assets were ~$219.9M; quarterly free cash flow burn is ~$87–93M, implying a cash runway of roughly 2–3 quarters absent new financing.
Q1 2026 free cash flow was $(93.3)M (improved from $(122.0)M in Q1 2025); Q2 2026 FCF guidance is $(87)M to $(92)M.
The company did NOT raise significant fresh cash capital in Q2 2026; it instead executed dilutive debt-for-equity swaps and modest ATM equity sales.

June 10, 2026: redeemed $30.5M principal of 9.80% First Lien Notes due 2028 by issuing 6,734,960 new shares (debt-for-equity); ~$172M of First Lien Notes remained, no principal due until March 31, 2028.
June 22 (closed ~June 29, 2026): exchanged ~$52.5M of 2.50% Convertible Notes due 2027 for stock/pre-funded warrants, cutting the 2027 Notes ~75% from $70.4M to $17.9M.
ATM program: ~$11M gross raised in Q1 2026 (4.0M shares) plus ~$52M more in April 2026; a new S-3 shelf registered ~$40.21M for future ATM offerings.
Share count rose from ~73.3M (Dec 31, 2025) to ~81.4M (Mar 31, 2026) and continued increasing through the June debt-for-equity transactions, reflecting ongoing dilution.
Investor Rich Huang / RichRich Capital disclosed a 5.26% stake (5.58M shares); a shareholder litigation settlement received preliminary court approval (~$2.75M from insurers).
SPCE traded ~$2.57 on July 10, 2026, down ~71% from its 52-week high of $8.90; market cap ~$205–330M. A June 1 spike to $8.90 (+125% in 5 sessions) was retail/momentum-driven.
Company survival is explicitly tied to commencing commercial spaceflight in Q4 2026 (flight testing targeted Q3 2026) with new Delta-class SpaceShips, plus continued capital-market access.
